Land Rover Discovery Sport P250 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery Sport P250 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned P250 engine (204PT) typically costs £2,200–£3,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,400. As a relatively modern engine, used and reconditioned supply is still developing – prices are indicative. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery Sport P250 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 204PT is a responsive turbo petrol from JLR's Ingenium family. However, it has known issues: the CVVL system can fail causing misfires, it's very sensitive to fuel quality (low-grade fuel can destroy pistons), and oil leaks are common. With proper maintenance and premium fuel, it's a capable engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Discovery Sport P250?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main issues are CVVL (Continuous Variable Valve Lift) system failure causing misfiring/rough running, fuel-quality sensitivity that can lead to piston damage, and oil leaks from the rear crankshaft seal and oil filter housing. The oil filter housing crack is a known recall issue.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery Sport P250 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Given the Discovery Sport's strong retained value (typically £25,000–£45,000+), a £2,200–£4,200 reconditioned engine is a modest fraction of the vehicle's worth. Replacement is almost always financially viable. Even at the higher end, it rarely exceeds our 60% Land Rover threshold against current market values.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Discovery Sport's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P250 uses the 204PT (also PT204 or AJ20P4) 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol. This is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The P250 badge itself confirms the engine – it's the 250 PS 2.0-litre petrol offered in the Discovery Sport L550 facelift.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair a P250 engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – CVVL system failure, oil leaks, ignition system wear – are repairs, not full replacements. The 204PT is a complex modern engine but specialists can address these issues. Major internal failure (piston damage from detonation, bottom-end failure) would typically justify replacement over rebuild.

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs; warranty claims; full provenance

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current Discovery Sport L550 P250 generation. As a relatively modern engine, supply of used and reconditioned units is developing — prices are indicative.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery Sport P250 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent Land Rover specialist).

cta.label: → Compare Discovery Sport P250 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: CVVL System Misfire / Rough Running (Ingenium Petrol)
problems.symptoms: Misfire, rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, engine warning light with Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) stored in the Powertrain Control Module (PCM).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored misfire can damage catalytic converter).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if engine warning light appears.

problems.recommendation: JLR has identified that the Continuous Variable Valve Lift (CVVL) system can fail on Ingenium I4 2.0-litre petrol engines, causing misfiring and rough running. Diagnosis typically involves following TOPIX 303-01 guidelines. If your Discovery Sport P250 is showing an engine warning light with misfire codes, have a specialist inspect the CVVL system promptly — replacement is the usual fix, and delaying can lead to further damage.

problems.title: Fuel Quality Sensitivity / Piston Damage
problems.symptoms: Detonation (pinking) under load, loss of power, engine knocking, eventual catastrophic failure.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — depends entirely on fuel quality.
problems.repairCost: £3,000–£6,000 [PLACEHOLDER] (engine rebuild or replacement if pistons destroyed).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear knocking or pinking, stop driving immediately.

problems.recommendation: The 204PT is a direct-injection turbo engine and is very demanding on fuel quality. The use of low-quality gasoline often leads to detonation and destruction of pistons. Always use the recommended high-octane fuel (Super unleaded, 97 RON or higher). If you hear any knocking or pinking under acceleration, stop driving immediately and have the engine inspected — piston damage is expensive and often terminal.

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Rear Crankshaft Seal / Oil Filter Housing)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the rear of the engine.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored oil loss can lead to engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: Leaks from under the rear crankshaft oil seal are common on the 204PT. Additionally, the oil filter housing may crack and cause oil to leak onto hot components — a known recall issue on some replacement engines. If you notice oil spots under your Discovery Sport or a burning oil smell, have the source identified immediately. Oil leaks can escalate quickly and, in the case of the oil filter housing, pose a fire risk.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery Sport P250 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Specialist repair — cheaper than engine replacement
Full engine replacement often more cost-effective than rebuild
Essential safety fix — may be covered by recall
Full engine replacement often more cost-effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Discovery Sport P250? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Land Rover service history with correct oil spec
* Evidence of premium fuel use (Super unleaded) — critical for this engine
* CVVL system checked and any recalls completed
* Warranty remaining or an extended warranty in place
avoidIf: * No evidence of premium fuel use — cheap fuel can destroy this engine
* Any sign of engine warning light or misfire codes
* Oil leaks from the rear of the engine or around the oil filter housing
* Poor service history — this engine demands strict maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Discovery Sport P250 offers a responsive 2.0-litre turbo petrol option for the compact SUV, with 250 PS and 365 Nm of torque. The Ingenium 204PT has known weaknesses — particularly the CVVL system and fuel-quality sensitivity. Buyers should prioritise vehicles with full service history and evidence of premium fuel use. When maintained correctly, it's a capable and refined engine; neglected examples can be expensive to repair.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery Sport P250 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery Sport P250 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Does it start cleanly? Any misfire or rough running?
2. Service History — Full Land Rover main dealer history with correct oil spec
3. Fuel History — Has the vehicle been run on premium unleaded (97 RON+)?
4. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any CVVL-related codes?
5. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the oil filter housing for any signs of leakage
6. Running Quality — Any hesitation, misfire, or rough idle when warm?
7. Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures or any advisory notes
